title: WebAssembly.compile presence check swallows all errors silently with no diagnostic
description: The `try/catch {}` block around the `wasm` detection at line 212-214 silently swallows any unexpected exception (e.g., SecurityError, TypeError). When it fires, `wasm` stays false and every downstream capability is zeroed out, but nothing is logged. Callers have no way to distinguish 'no WASM support' from 'exception during detection'. The comment at C-9 acknowledges CSP issues but doesn't address the silent failure path.

title: createImageBitmap JXL probe has no timeout and can hang indefinitely
description: `createImageBitmap(blob)` is awaited without any timeout at line 190. On some browsers or runtime environments this call may never resolve (e.g., if the browser queues decoding tasks on a blocked compositor thread, or the MIME type is unrecognised and the implementation hangs). Because `_capsPromise` is memoised, a single hung probe permanently blocks all future `getCapabilities()` callers in that page lifetime. A `Promise.race` with a short timeout (e.g. 500 ms) would contain the blast radius.

title: probeWebGpuAdapter has no timeout — gpu.requestAdapter() can stall indefinitely
description: `gpu.requestAdapter()` is awaited without a timeout at line 285. The WebGPU spec permits implementations to return a promise that resolves only after a user gesture or after driver enumeration, which can take several seconds on some systems. The memoised `_gpuAdapterPromise` means all subsequent `probeWebGpuAdapter()` callers block for the same unbounded duration. A `Promise.race` with an explicit deadline (e.g. 2 s) would protect callers.

title: Dynamic import of @casabio/jxl-native swallows all errors including non-ModuleNotFound ones
description: The bare `catch {}` at line 247 swallows every failure from `import('@casabio/jxl-native')` — including transient I/O errors, SyntaxErrors in the module, and ABI mismatches — silently setting `nativeJxlDecoder = false`. A native module that fails due to a corrupted binary or version mismatch will be silently skipped, leaving the caller with no diagnostic. At minimum, non-`ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` / `MODULE_NOT_FOUND` errors should be re-thrown or at least logged.

title: probeWebGpuAdapter accesses navigator without existence guard
description: Inside the IIFE at line 283, `(navigator as any)?.gpu` is accessed without first checking `typeof navigator !== 'undefined'`. In a non-browser Worker context or in Node.js, `navigator` is undefined at module scope and the optional-chain `?.gpu` will throw a ReferenceError before the optional chain can help, because `navigator` is not a property access but a bare identifier. The `computeCapabilities` function does guard `navigator` (line 235–238) but `probeWebGpuAdapter` is a separately exported function that can be called independently.

title: ImageDecoder.isTypeSupported result is not narrowed before boolean coercion — a non-boolean truthy return goes unchecked
description: `ID.isTypeSupported('image/jxl')` is awaited and used as a boolean via `if (await ...)`. The WebCodecs spec defines the return as `Promise<boolean>`, but browser implementations have historically returned other truthy values. More critically, if `isTypeSupported` returns `true` the function immediately returns `true` without verifying that `ImageDecoder` can actually instantiate — a browser could support the type string check but fail at decode time. This is a minor accuracy concern for the probe rather than a crash risk.

title: No structured diagnostic channel: all probe failures are silently swallowed with no observability hook
description: All four `_probe*` functions, plus the native-module import and the `createImageBitmap` probe, use bare `catch {}` or `catch { /* fall through */ }` with no way for callers to discover what failed. In production this makes it impossible to differentiate 'SIMD disabled by browser flag' from 'WebAssembly.validate threw unexpectedly'. An optional `onDiagnostic?: (event: CapabilityDiagnostic) => void` callback on `getCapabilities()` or a debug-mode console path would let integrators surface silent failures without breaking the current API contract.

title: No shared withTimeout utility — two async probes independently need timeout protection
description: `probeNativeJxl` (createImageBitmap) and `probeWebGpuAdapter` (requestAdapter) are the two async probes in this module and both currently lack timeout protection (errors-2 and errors-3). A small `withTimeout<T>(p: Promise<T>, ms: number, fallback: T): Promise<T>` utility — three lines of Promise.race — would let both probes be guarded consistently and prevent any future async probe from shipping without one.
